meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the blank page syndrome. Learn to build a professional SaaS starter kit using Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for student portfolios.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis ends by shifting focus from unique concepts to execution velocity. By leveraging a robust SaaS starter kit, you build a production-ready foundation—auth, payments, and database—allowing you to rapidly prototype real-world solutions that actually solve problems for users, instantly upgrading your portfolio from theoretical to professional.
content.intro:
The blank editor window is the graveyard of engineering ambition. You have the technical skills to build, but you are paralyzed by the search for a 'unicorn' idea that never comes. This stagnation is the silent killer of student careers, keeping you trapped in tutorial hell while others ship products and secure roles.

Today, we pivot. Instead of waiting for inspiration, we build the infrastructure. By mastering a SaaS starter kit, you stop being a student and start being a founder-engineer. We will use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to accelerate your implementation, turning your first project into a high-fidelity asset that proves you can build, deploy, and scale.

steps.title: Architecting the Foundation with Antigravity
steps.description: Define the core requirements of your SaaS. Focus on the 'holy trinity' of SaaS: User Auth, Stripe integration, and a relational database schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable Next.js project architecture for a SaaS starter kit. Include modules for Supabase integration, Stripe webhook handling, and a modular component library structure.

steps.title: Implementing Secure Authentication
steps.description: Authentication is the first hurdle. Use your starter kit to integrate OAuth and session management, ensuring users can sign up and secure their data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust authentication flow using Supabase Auth. Include protected routes for user dashboards and middleware for session validation.

steps.title: Integrating Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: A SaaS isn't a project without billing. Set up Stripe to handle subscriptions, ensuring you understand how to manage customer lifecycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a Stripe webhook handler to sync subscription status with the Supabase users table. Ensure it handles 'subscription.created' and 'subscription.deleted' events.

steps.title: Building the Core Feature Engine
steps.description: Now that the plumbing is done, build the feature that actually solves a problem. Use Claude Code to iterate on the UI/UX rapidly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CRUD interface for a 'Task' resource. Include server-side validation and optimistic UI updates using React Query.

steps.title: Deploying to Production
steps.description: Move your local project to the internet. Testing production-ready configurations is what separates hobbyists from professionals.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment manifest and CI/CD pipeline configuration for Vercel, including environment variable injection for production secrets.

pro_tips: Never hardcode environment variables; use a .env.example file to define your schema without exposing credentials.
Prioritize 'feature toggles' early so you can ship a partial product without breaking the user experience.
Focus on one 'hero' feature rather than building ten half-baked utilities; depth impresses recruiters more than breadth.
Write automated tests for your checkout flow first; if users can't pay, you don't have a business.
Treat your GitHub repo as a product showcase: include a high-quality README with screenshots and a clear 'How to run' section.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, identify one niche audience (e.g., local coffee shops, freelance writers) and build a specific tool for them using your new foundation.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea. You have the tools, the architecture, and the roadmap to build a professional-grade SaaS right now. Every line of code you write today is a step toward your future as a high-impact developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
